channel.title: Skiing on My AI Digest
channel.description: Recent content in Skiing on My AI Digest

item.title: Backcountry Issue166 A Quiet Place Summary
item.description:
Generated by Codex with GPT-5

What this article is really about

This is a ski-travel story that turns into a conservation story. Liam McGee heads to Villa Cerro Castillo in Chile’s Aysen region expecting the usual dream pitch for Patagonia: roadside access, steep lines, and a frontier full of first descents. Instead, the crew gets a historically lean snow year, long bushwhacks, awkward moss skiing, and just enough good turns to keep the vision alive. That mismatch is the point. The article argues that the place matters more than the conditions because Cerro Castillo’s real value is not just ski potential, but the fact that so much of it remains intact.
